reasoning: According to multiple recent sources published in late 2024 and 2025, the financial inclusion rate in Nigeria (which includes both bank and non-bank accounts) only reached approximately 64% by 2024. This is substantially below the Central Bank of Nigeria's ambitious target of 95%. Given the consistent reporting of a rate in the mid-60s, there is virtually no chance that 95% of Nigerians had a bank account before 2025. I am therefore predicting the lowest possible probability of 0.02.
